nodes.name: Psalidoprocne
nodes.summary: Psalidoprocne（Psalidoprocne）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联5种鸟类，如Square-tailed saw-wing、Mountain saw-wing、White-headed saw-wing等5种鸟类。

nodes.name: Fanti saw-wing
nodes.summary: Fanti saw-wing（Psalidoprocne obscura）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Fanti saw-wing。

nodes.name: Fanti saw-wing
nodes.summary: The Fanti saw-wing (Psalidoprocne obscura), also known as the Fanti rough-winged swallow, is a small passerine bird in the swallow family. The Fanti saw-wing is often described as the most beautiful of the swallows, owing to its uniformly shimmering green plumage. Description This is a small swallow at 17 cm. Its plumage is glossy green. The tail is long and deeply forked. Sexes are similar, but the female has a shorter tail. Juveniles are brown with little gloss, and have short tails. Distribution and habitat The Fanti saw-wing breeds in the lowlands of southern west Africa from Senegal to Cameroon. It is mainly resident, apart from seasonal movements. This bird is found in open country, including light woodland, near water. Fanti saw-wings are graceful flyers and they generally feed on insects, including beetles, while airborne. They are typically seen low over water or grassland. The lined nests are built in a 60 cm burrow in a vertical bank. The clutch is two eggs. References Birds of The Gambia by Barlow, Wacher and Disley, ISBN 1-873403-32-1 Swallows and Martins by Turner and Rose ISBN 0-7470-3202-5
